programme.desc: Host Laura Rutledge takes the helm of this weekday show featuring the likes of Marcus Spears, Dan Orlovsky, Mina Kimes, and Adam Schefter, who offer the latest news and analysis from across the NFL.

programme.title: The Last Dance
programme.desc: With Michael Jordan's extraordinary fame came extraordinary scrutiny, and in 1993, it all felt like it came to a head with the Chicago Bulls' pursuit of a third straight title shadowed by question marks about Jordan's gambling.

programme.title: The Last Dance
programme.desc: As Michael Jordan pushes his teammates hard for a sixth NBA title, his intensity and hunger for basketball dominance is in stark contrast to his mindset in October 1993, when he stunned the world by walking away from the game.

programme.title: The Last Dance
programme.desc: Michael Jordan would famously use even the smallest slight imaginable to motivate himself against an opponent. And when his NBA comeback in 1995 fell short in the playoffs, he worked harder than ever to return to form.

programme.title: Chopped
programme.desc: The chefs turn PB&J pancakes into appetizers; a rainbow ingredient in the entrée basket is not a sunny surprise for the chefs; ice cream drama in the dessert round has the judges on the edge of their seats.

programme.title: Chopped
programme.desc: In the first round, the chefs get cracking to procure crab meat to make their appetizers; a strange dip in the second round is a big part of the challenge, while the dessert basket is filled with plenty of fun, including mocktails and fruit balls.

programme.title: Chopped
programme.desc: Four chefs compete in a battle celebrating Chinese cuisine; they scramble to distinguish themselves when prompted to make similar appetizers, showcase their unique style with an impressive salmon entrée and craft a dessert from noodles and candy.

programme.title: Chopped
programme.desc: Four new chefs are thrown into the mix as they try to transform a hearty sandwich into a dainty appetizer; in the second round, the competitors must make creative, beautiful entrees with weird soda and quail.

programme.title: Chopped
programme.desc: The chefs find a peculiar cereal and fluke fillets in the first basket; the entrée round ingredients run the gamut from light to hearty and the finalists must work a clever crêpe and an interactive candy into their desserts.

programme.title: Chopped
programme.desc: With no major tricks in the first basket, the chefs are left to create their own problems, as they attempt to piece together meat and potato appetizers; in the entrée round, duck dishes are a tall order in just 30 minutes.

programme.title: Chopped Castaways

programme.desc: The chefs find a salty veggie and a dried fruit in the appetizer basket; in the entrée round, a carnival treat meets a Tex-Mex staple in the form of a strange mashup ingredient; chefs come up with ideas for creative cakes in the final round.

programme.desc: Mary resorts to drastic measures to earn George an opportunity to impress the King; George must pull out all the stops and go head-to-head with Earl Somerset in order to stand out and prove himself to the King.

programme.desc: Detectives investigate when a man is killed by a car bomb before he can disconnect his incapacitated wife's feeding tube.

programme.desc: Detectives learn that a murdered prostitute was actually an undercover reporter trying to expose a brutal gang.

programme.desc: A nurse practitioner with a social agenda faces charges when an abusive young mother accused of murder suddenly dies in her jail cell; detectives learn she may have been sterilized, without knowing it.

programme.desc: Religious tempers flare when a man is killed after desecrating a treasured edition of the Hebrew Torah.
